must_have_breakdown.skill: System Requirements writing and System Architecture Design
RADAR System (End User function) and Product knowledge
Experience on system service function (Diagnostics, Flashability, FOTA, etc) development
Basic Functional Safety and Cyber Security knowledge
Experience in RADAR function validation (HIL and vehicle environment)
Requirement management tools DOORs/Code Beamer and configuration management tools such as CoreALM

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Platform Manager and DevOps Engineer with 6+ years of enterprise CRM delivery experience across banking and healthcare verticals. The role requires an automotive systems engineer with deep RADAR domain expertise, ASPICE/ISO26262 functional safety knowledge, embedded systems experience, automotive communication protocols (CAN/LIN/Ethernet/LVDS), and hands-on HIL/vehicle validation capability. Not a single must-have skill from the JD is present in this resume. There is zero overlap between Salesforce ecosystem management and automotive radar system engineering. Do not progress this candidate under any circumstances — this is a fundamental domain mismatch, not a skill gap.
strengths: COPADO CI/CD MASTERY: Managed 50+ end-to-end Salesforce releases at HSBC with zero unplanned production outages over 18 months across 5 environments — exceptional release discipline for a CRM DevOps role.

ENTERPRISE PLATFORM GOVERNANCE: Established Center of Excellence CI/CD frameworks and release governance SOPs adopted as NTT Data standard across multiple engagements, demonstrating strategic platform ownership beyond individual delivery.

QUANTIFIED DELIVERY OUTCOMES: Reduced deployment cycle time by 35% at FinSure, production defect escape rate by 30% and deployment time by 25% at CVS Health — strong evidence of measurable operational impact.
gaps: ZERO AUTOMOTIVE/RADAR DOMAIN KNOWLEDGE: The entire resume is Salesforce CRM and DevOps — there is no evidence of embedded systems, RADAR product knowledge, ASPICE, ISO26262, CAN/LIN/Ethernet protocols, or any automotive systems engineering experience. This is a disqualifying domain mismatch.

NO SYSTEMS ENGINEERING OR FUNCTIONAL SAFETY BACKGROUND: The role requires requirements writing (DOORs/CodeBeamer), system architecture design, HIL validation, and functional safety — none of which appear anywhere in this candidate's profile. There is no remedial path without a complete career pivot.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period specified in resume and no JD maximum stated — no penalty applied.
JD requires in-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Hyderabad, India — location mismatch.
Candidate has 6+ years total experience, which exceeds half of the 4-year relevant experience requirement — no penalty on raw years alone.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference specified in JD — no penalty applied.

hard_filter_failures: Location mismatch — candidate is in Hyderabad, role requires in-office presence in Chennai
availability_reason: Latest role listed end date as March 2026 — candidate appears to be between roles or recently concluded an engagement.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce Platform Manager / Salesforce DevOps Lead
rare_assets: Salesforce Premier Support escalation experience at HSBC — direct enterprise-tier vendor engagement is uncommon at this career level.
CoE framework establishment adopted as NTT Data standard across multiple global engagements — demonstrates org-wide platform influence beyond project scope.
self_reported_unverified: Claim of 'zero unplanned production outages over 18 months' at HSBC — a strong outcome claim with no third-party corroboration or incident log reference.
'Zero-finding compliance audits' across all internal and external banking audits — impressive but entirely self-reported with no audit body or report cited.
CoE framework 'adopted as the standard across NTT Data Salesforce engagements' — organisational adoption claim without evidence of formal sign-off or scale of adoption.
interview_flags: Probe why a B.Com graduate is in a highly technical platform engineering role — clarify self-taught path and any formal technical training beyond Salesforce certifications.
Ask whether candidate has any exposure to automotive, embedded systems, or safety-critical engineering — confirm zero overlap before closing the file.
Verify the end date of March 2026 on current role — unusual future date; confirm whether actively employed, on notice, or already available.
Probe actual depth of 'architecture' work — distinguish between configuring Copado pipelines versus true system architecture design decisions.
Clarify team size managed or led — 'delivered knowledge transfer to 10+ developers' is the only people-leadership signal; confirm whether this was formal management or informal mentoring.
